mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances and their resources in Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer to manage their observability stack using Kubernetes-native tools and GitOps workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and the potential complexity of managing custom resources.

steps.title: Add the Grafana Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Grafana Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Grafana Operator.

```bash

helm repo add grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts

```

> This command sets up the repository for future installations.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator in your Kubernetes cluster using the specified version.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Use the specific version to ensure compatibility.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This YAML manifest defines a Grafana instance and a sample dashboard. Apply it to your cluster to create the resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: <REDACTED>
---
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: GrafanaDashboard
metadata:
  name: sample-dashboard
spec:
  resyncPeriod: 30s
  instanceSelector:
    matchLabels:
      dashboards: "grafana"
  json: >
    {
      "title": "Simple Dashboard",
      "timezone": "browser",
      "refresh": "5s",
      "panels": [],
      "time": {
        "from": "now-6h",
        "to": "now"
      }
    }
EOF

```

> This creates both a Grafana instance and a sample dashboard.

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
Check the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Look for 'status' to confirm the instance is ready.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete your Grafana instances and dashboards, leading to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> This command will remove the operator from your cluster.
> This command deletes the Grafana instance and its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Grafana configurations. The upgrade process involves upgrading the Helm release and verifying the instance status.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backup the current Grafana configuration.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the Grafana instance is still running correctly after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Grafana instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana Operator for any errors: kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=grafana-operator

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Grafana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is created and check the service type: kubectl get svc grafana

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized access'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The admin credentials may not be set correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the admin credentials in the Grafana CR and update if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dashboard not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The GrafanaDashboard CR may not have been applied correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the GrafanaDashboard: kubectl get grafanadashboard sample-dashboard -o yaml

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the status 'ready' when executing: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that you have the necessary tools installed.
